# omni-infra-provider-truenas

> TrueNAS SCALE infrastructure provider for the Sidero Omni Kubernetes management platform.

Automatically provisions and manages Talos Linux virtual machines on TrueNAS SCALE through Sidero Omni. When Omni requests a machine (user scales a cluster), this provider creates a VM on TrueNAS with ZFS-backed storage and automatic cluster enrollment.

## Key Facts

- Language: Go
- License: MIT
- Author: Bear Binary
- Requires: TrueNAS SCALE 25.04+ (Fangtooth), Sidero Omni instance
- API: TrueNAS JSON-RPC 2.0 (NOT REST v2.0)
- Transports: Unix socket (zero-auth, on TrueNAS) or WebSocket (API key, remote)
- Container: ghcr.io/bearbinary/omni-infra-provider-truenas
- Architectures: amd64, arm64

## What It Does

1. Watches Omni for MachineRequests (user scales a cluster)
2. Generates a Talos Linux image schematic with extensions (qemu-guest-agent, nfs-utils, util-linux-tools)
3. Downloads the Talos ISO from Image Factory (cached, SHA-256 deduplicated)
4. Creates a VM on TrueNAS: zvol for disk, CDROM with ISO, NIC on bridge/VLAN
5. VM boots Talos, joins Omni via SideroLink (outbound WireGuard)
6. On removal: stops VM, deletes VM and zvol

## Configuration

All via environment variables:

- OMNI_ENDPOINT (required): Omni instance URL
- OMNI_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_KEY (required): Omni infra provider key
- TRUENAS_HOST: TrueNAS hostname (WebSocket only)
- TRUENAS_API_KEY: TrueNAS API key (WebSocket only)
- DEFAULT_POOL: ZFS pool for zvols (default: "default")
- DEFAULT_NIC_ATTACH: Network interface for VM NICs
- DEFAULT_BOOT_METHOD: UEFI or BIOS (default: UEFI)
- CONCURRENCY: Max parallel workers (default: 4)

## MachineClass Config (configpatch)

- cpus (int, required): Virtual CPUs, min 1
- memory (int, required): Memory in MiB, min 1024
- disk_size (int, required): Root disk in GiB, min 10
- pool (string): ZFS pool override
- nic_attach (string): Network interface override
- boot_method (string): UEFI or BIOS
- architecture (string): amd64 or arm64
- extensions (list): Additional Talos system extensions

## Deployment Options

1. TrueNAS App (recommended): Docker container with Unix socket mounted, no API key
2. Kubernetes: Kustomize manifests in deploy/kubernetes/
3. Docker Compose: Remote deployment via WebSocket in deploy/docker-compose.yaml

## For Beginners

docs/getting-started.md is a complete 6-step tutorial for users new to Kubernetes:
1. Sign up for Omni (free tier available for homelab)
2. Prepare TrueNAS (check version, identify ZFS pool, create network bridge)
3. Install the provider as a TrueNAS app
4. Create MachineClasses and first Kubernetes cluster via Omni
5. Install kubectl, download kubeconfig, verify nodes
6. Deploy a test app (nginx) and access it via NodePort

Includes: Windows/macOS/Linux instructions, inline troubleshooting at each step, hardware sizing guide, 13-question FAQ (cost, performance, disk usage, internet requirements, SSH, reboots), and a 20-term glossary (bridge, zvol, DHCP, kubeconfig, Helm, pod, etc.).

## Project Structure

- cmd/omni-infra-provider-truenas/main.go: Entry point
- internal/client/: TrueNAS JSON-RPC 2.0 client (transport, VM, storage, device ops)
- internal/provisioner/: 3-step provision flow + deprovision
- internal/cleanup/: Background stale resource cleanup
- internal/resources/: COSI typed resources
- internal/telemetry/: OpenTelemetry integration
- api/specs/: Protobuf definitions
- deploy/: Docker Compose, Kubernetes, observability configs

## Documentation

- README.md: Full setup and usage guide with badges, diagrams, and config reference
- AGENT.md: Step-by-step deployment guide designed for AI assistants walking users through setup
- CLAUDE.md: AI assistant context for developing this codebase
- CONTRIBUTING.md: Issues-only contribution model (no PRs accepted)
- SECURITY.md: Vulnerability disclosure policy
- CHANGELOG.md: Curated release history (v0.1.0 through current)
- docs/architecture.md: Detailed architecture with Mermaid diagrams
- docs/networking.md: Networking guide — bridges, DHCP reservations, MetalLB, VIP, UniFi/pfSense/OPNsense/Mikrotik
- docs/storage.md: CSI storage guide — NFS, iSCSI, democratic-csi, node-local options
- docs/cni.md: CNI selection guide — Flannel, Cilium, Calico with Talos-specific setup
- docs/troubleshooting.md: Common issues and solutions
- docs/testing.md: Unit, integration, and E2E test setup
- docs/getting-started.md: Beginner tutorial from NAS to running Kubernetes cluster
- docs/upgrading.md: Version upgrade guide and breaking changes
- docs/provider-research.md: Internal research on all existing Omni provider implementations
- docs/backlog.md: Feature roadmap
- .env.example: Environment variable reference
- llms-full.txt: Complete AI reference (same as AGENT.md)
